original_prompt:
PORTFOLIO_CHILD_PROJECT: true
Parent portfolio: batch-games
Selected app slug: longwave

Build this app as a working local-first SwiftUI MVP.

## Selected App Spec

# Longwave

Build mode: **MVP build**.

## App Name

Longwave

## Category

idle

## One-Sentence Promise

Build-and-optimize radio-relay network idle game

## Claude Design Handoff Prompt

(not supplied yet)

## Parent Portfolio Prompt

Build 8 completely separate production-ready iOS games at the same time: exactly 2 unique idle games, 2 unique puzzle games, 2 drinking/party games playable by a group passing around ONE phone, and 2 large-group games where up to 10-12 players each connect from their own phone to one shared session (card-game formats welcome; prefer local peer-to-peer / GameKit / MultipeerConnectivity over a cloud backend so they stay local-first).

This is a multi-app PORTFOLIO request: multiple apps in one program. Each selected app becomes its own separate project — one folder per app.


Requirements for every app:
1. Must go from 0 to production-ready, not just a few features.
2. Must be unique, useful, and commercially viable.
3. Must be beautifully designed and feel premium.
4. Must provide real end-user value.
5. Must have a realistic path to monetization — a subscription or paid offering with genuine value (never cosmetics-only).
6. Should have viral potential (broad or niche) without sacrificing usefulness.
7. Must be better than its competitors in a meaningful way.
8. Local-first where applicable, architected so cloud support can be added later without rewriting.

UNIQUENESS (hard rule, non-negotiable):
- No app in this batch may be similar to another app in this batch.
- No app may be similar to anything already built or being built in this workspace or before, including: Waylay (location-based personal recall utility), TrueScale, Provenance, Scope-Guard, Practice-Loop, Proof, ReturnWise, VerveCoach, CueKeeper, Countertop, FieldQuote, my existing digital temple app, and my existing PO-automation tool.
- No app may be similar to apps in the OTHER batches of this program (check sibling project folders in the workspace for their concepts).
- Do not reuse prior concepts, themes, or mechanics from earlier apps — even if the request sounds similar to something built before, produce a differentiated concept.

Bonus points (optional — only when integral and genuinely valuable, never forced): unique use of LLMs, AR, or ML.

Build rules:
- Run the app efforts in parallel; keep discussion/design phases separate per app.
- Each app gets its own full product direction, design direction, architecture, implementation plan, and build output.
- Save all phase discussions and final decisions; at the end combine each app's full transcript into a .txt file.
- If an app is liked, also prepare Jira board + Notion project structures for backfilling later.
- Use enough rounds per phase for high-quality discussion, ending early on consensus.
- Recover cleanly from stalls; report only important milestones.

Output:
- One folder per app in the output directory — never one folder containing all apps.
Output directory:
- /Users/pchordia/Documents/iOS-App-Factory

Additional notes:
Monetization must sell REAL value (content, modes, mechanics, utility) — never cosmetics/skins alone.

Now generate at least 16 candidate ideas, discuss them thoroughly, choose the best 8, and build all 8 to production quality.


## Change requested
The app currently FAILS to compile. Fix every compiler error until the build succeeds cleanly; do not drop features unless unavoidable.

phase_discussions.title: Scope the change
phase_discussions.output: You've hit your monthly spend limit · raise it at claude.ai/settings/usage

tasks.title: Package scaffold and shared domain types
acceptance_criteria: swift build succeeds with zero warnings treated as errors
every type/signature matches the frozen tech_specs interfaces-json exactly, including the .engineNotReady PlacementError case
empty LongwaveSimulationTests target runs
no SwiftUI/UIKit import anywhere in this package
no direct Date() calls anywhere in this package
on completion, post the one-sentence contract: which signatures are now frozen for services_utilities and primary_ui to build against

tasks.title: Interference, throughput, economy math, and validate()
acceptance_criteria: interference is continuous with a minimum-distance floor: no NaN/Infinity at close range, no step-function cliffs
identical/near-identical coordinate placement is cleanly rejected via PlacementError, never crashes
exactly one currency type exists anywhere in the model
seed content defines the free region's static/finite demand zones at fixed coordinates
validate(_:) rejects overlapping/out-of-bounds coordinates, negative currency, and unrecognized schemaVersion

tasks.title: Closed-form offline resolution and clock policy
acceptance_criteria: resolveOffline implements earnings = steadyStateRate x min(elapsed, cap) exactly, clock always injected via ClockProviding
two-threshold rule implemented: sub-drift deltas floor silently to zero, deltas beyond the tier cap clamp with wasCapped=true, everything between is plain math
no iterative tick-by-tick catch-up loop anywhere in this function

tasks.title: LongwaveSimulation full deterministic test suite
acceptance_criteria: 8-hour and 400+ day simulated offline gaps both return finite, formula-matching values via injected fake clock
clock-drift floor/cap thresholds each have a passing test
fixture test proves a small relay move produces a small derived-state delta
fixture test proves placing a second relay near a first visibly degrades health/throughput
all tests run with zero simulator dependency
on merge to longwave/integration, post the fixture data and expected outputs services_utilities and primary_ui can build against

tasks.title: SnapshotStoring protocol and atomic SnapshotStore
acceptance_criteria: writes are temp-file-then-rename, never write-in-place
lastKnownGood.json promotion is gated by calling validate(_:) first, decode success alone is not sufficient
corrupted files are preserved under a timestamped quarantine filename before falling back
load() returns LoadResult carrying a RecoveryNotice whenever recovery occurred, never silently swallowed
markLastActive(at:) is called from scenePhase-to-background wiring, not only clean termination

tasks.title: EntitlementProviding, StoreKitEntitlementService, resolveRegionAccess, privacy manifest
acceptance_criteria: either the lifetime product ID or the subscription satisfies Pro, never hardcoded to just one
Transaction.updates listener task starts at construction time, not lazily on first refresh()
entitlement reads only from StoreKit's local transaction cache, never blocks on a live network call
resolveRegionAccess is the single named function producing RegionAccessState from EntitlementState plus hasLocalRegionTwoData(), with a test matrix covering all four combinations
restoredButLocallyEmpty is derived from local region-two save presence, never from receipt/entitlement presence alone
PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy declares required-reason API usage for UserDefaults and file-timestamp access covering both this file's StoreKit caching and T-005's persistence layer

tasks.title: HistoryRetentionService
acceptance_criteria: append() enforces retentionLimit as a hard cap regardless of entitlement duration
history entries are summary-grade (start/end/health/bottlenecks), never raw per-tick data
buffer is retained (hidden, not deleted) when entitlement lapses

tasks.title: Services integration test suite
acceptance_criteria: 20 consecutive save/relaunch cycles including one force-quit mid-placement and one mid-upgrade produce zero corruption/duplication/silent reset, run against a real temp-directory FileManager
corrupted-save quarantine-then-rollback verified against a real on-disk broken file, not a mock
all four entitlement states exercised against the real .storekit config, including restore in airplane mode
on merge to longwave/integration, post which concrete SnapshotStoring/EntitlementProviding implementations and failure-mode fixtures primary_ui can now build against

tasks.title: Xcode project scaffold, NetworkEngine, and action/presentation contracts
acceptance_criteria: project uses file-system-synchronized groups for the app target, package, and both test targets so lanes never hand-edit project.pbxproj after this task
project.pbxproj becomes a forbidden-edit zone for every other lane from this point forward; new targets/schemes route through a named request to this lane
attemptPlace/attemptMove/attemptRetune/attemptUpgrade are fully synchronous end-to-end, no await between validate and mutate
each attempt* method returns .engineNotReady before bootstrap() completes rather than mutating state or crashing
milestone flags (firstRelayPlaced, firstInterferenceResolved, third goal) are recomputed automatically and idempotently (latch false-to-true only) after every successful attempt* call
fixture initializer produces a fully usable NetworkEngine with no real FileManager/StoreKit access, for Previews/XCTest/XCUITest
primary_ui becomes the standing integrator for all milestone merges from longwave/integration into main from this point forward

tasks.title: App entry point and scene-phase lifecycle wiring
acceptance_criteria: bootstrap() runs on launch and never blocks first frame render on a live network call
handleScenePhaseChange writes lastActiveAt via markLastActive on transition to .background
no BackgroundTasks / BGTaskScheduler usage anywhere

tasks.title: Canvas render surface and map hit-testing/accessibility overlay
acceptance_criteria: the live signal/coverage/interference layer is one Canvas driven by one TimelineView(.animation) fed by plain structs, never a ForEach of individually animated shapes
MapInteractionNode uses the exact same screenPosition conversion as the Canvas renderer so the visible relay dot and its tap target never drift apart
renders correctly at at least one small and one large region size without dropped frames in a manual check

tasks.title: Map screen and build/inspect bottom sheet
acceptance_criteria: every value shown (health, throughput, currency) is read from NetworkDerivedState, never a second/shadow calculation
empty, healthy, strained, choking, placement-preview, selected-relay, offline-return, and recovery-banner states are all implemented and reachable
invalid-placement feedback is persistent, not a flash, and includes a stated reason
health/interference indicators carry a redundant non-color signal
insufficient-funds state disables the confirm action with a stated reason rather than a dead tap

tasks.title: Progression, paywall, and settings screens
acceptance_criteria: progression screen's health value and offline-earnings figure come from the same NetworkDerivedState/OfflineSummary the map uses, verified equal in a test, not a second computation
all four Pro entitlement UI states (never-purchased, active, restored-but-locally-empty, lapsed-frozen) are each a distinct, implemented visual state
restore purchases has its own loading/success/error states
from cold start or resume-with-offline-earnings, the next actionable goal is identifiable without opening a second screen

tasks.title: Engine and UI integration test suite
acceptance_criteria: 10 concurrent attemptPlace/attemptUpgrade calls against a fixture engine produce exactly one currency deduction
attemptPlace called before bootstrap() resolves returns .engineNotReady, does not crash or mutate state
resolveRegionAccess(.notOwned, false) renders lockedUpsell end-to-end through the real UI, not just the pure function
milestone flags flip automatically without a separate explicit call and never un-flip on a later action

tasks.title: VoiceOver custom actions for relays and demand zones
acceptance_criteria: move N/S/E/W, retune up/down, upgrade, and commit/cancel are all exposed as custom actions routed through the same NetworkActioning entry points as drag gestures, never a separate calculation path
each action speaks the projected health/output delta from the same PlacementOutcome the visual delta chip uses, before commit
XCUITest asserts the actions exist and route correctly

tasks.title: Recovery messaging wired into map and settings
acceptance_criteria: every RecoveryNotice case maps to a calm, plain-language string, never raw technical failure text
recovery banner appears on the map only when a RecoveryNotice is present, and the same message is available on the settings screen

tasks.title: Dynamic Type and non-color signaling audit
acceptance_criteria: at largest accessibility Dynamic Type size, the build/inspect sheet and all screens neither clip nor break any control
no health/interference/error state anywhere conveys meaning by color alone
any fix required in a primary_ui-owned file is delivered as a named patch reviewed and merged by the primary_ui owner, never a direct commit

tasks.title: Final hardening: stress re-run, atomicity, performance
acceptance_criteria: 20-cycle save/relaunch stress re-run against the finished app produces zero corruption/duplication/silent reset
rapid double-tap on upgrade/placement actions never double-spends
Canvas render surface holds acceptable frame pacing with a full region of relays placed

tasks.title: Scripted first-session walkthrough and ship gate
acceptance_criteria: walkthrough verifies, using must-have features only: first signal flow success, visible self-inflicted interference from a second relay, recovery via a smarter move, and a legible fourth objective
if the fourth objective doesn't emerge from must-haves alone, the milestone-gated second demand zone is promoted to must-have and T-002's seed content is updated accordingly before ship
one real manual VoiceOver pass is completed and its result recorded, not just XCUITest action-routing checks

interfaces.name: GameSnapshot
interfaces.signature: struct GameSnapshot: Codable, Sendable { var schemaVersion: Int; var saveSequence: Int; var savedAtWallClock: Date; var lastActiveAt: Date; var regions: [RegionID: RegionTopology]; var currency: Decimal; var milestones: MilestoneState; var proHistory: ProHistoryBuffer; var lastOfflineResolution: OfflineSummary? }
interfaces.notes: Canonical persisted document only; no derived throughput/health fields ever stored.

interfaces.name: RegionState
interfaces.signature: struct RegionState: Codable, Sendable, Identifiable { var id: RegionID; var definitionID: RegionDefinitionID; var demandZones: [DemandZone]; var relays: [Relay]; var unlockedMilestones: Set<MilestoneID>; var accessState: RegionAccessState }
interfaces.notes: Holds mutable per-region topology and entitlement-driven freeze state.

interfaces.name: Relay
interfaces.signature: struct Relay: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var type: RelayType; var position: Coordinate; var tuningLevel: Int; var upgradeLevel: Int }
interfaces.notes: Single bounded Int tuning lever (LOCKED), matching the single 'retune up/down' accessibility action — a two-axis continuous tuning space would be reachable by drag but not by one VoiceOver stepper.

interfaces.name: DemandZone
interfaces.signature: struct DemandZone: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var position: Coordinate; var demandWeight: Double }
interfaces.notes: Static/finite per region; count changes only via milestone unlock.

interfaces.name: RelayType
interfaces.signature: enum RelayType: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case broad, focused, stabilizer }
interfaces.notes: Exactly three MVP types; a fourth Pro type, if built, is additive.

interfaces.name: RelayTuning
interfaces.signature: struct RelayTuning: Codable, Sendable { var powerBias: Double; var channelOffset: Double }
interfaces.notes: Bounded tuning inputs used for deterministic preview and commit flows.

interfaces.name: NetworkDerivedState
interfaces.signature: struct NetworkDerivedState: Sendable { var healthLevel: HealthLevel; var healthScore: Double; var totalThroughput: Double; var steadyStateCreditsPerSecond: Decimal; var relayMetrics: [RelayMetric]; var bottleneckRelayIDs: [UUID] }
interfaces.notes: The single contract read by map meter, progression screen, VoiceOver deltas, and Pro history; never duplicated or cached elsewhere.

interfaces.name: NetworkHealth
interfaces.signature: enum NetworkHealth: String, Sendable { case healthy; case strained; case choking }
interfaces.notes: One authoritative health state used everywhere user-visible.

interfaces.name: OfflineResolutionSummary
interfaces.signature: struct OfflineResolutionSummary: Codable, Sendable { var elapsedSeconds: TimeInterval; var clampedSeconds: TimeInterval; var creditedAmount: Decimal; var wasCapped: Bool; var note: OfflineNote? }
interfaces.notes: Persist latest resume result for progression screen and return overlay.

interfaces.name: GoalProgress
interfaces.signature: struct GoalProgress: Codable, Sendable { var placedFirstRelay: Bool; var resolvedFirstInterference: Bool; var reachedThirdObjective: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Compact checklist state only, not a generic quest system.

interfaces.name: ProHistoryBuffer
interfaces.signature: struct ProHistoryBuffer: Codable, Sendable { var entries: [HistoryEntry]; var retentionLimit: Int }
interfaces.notes: Capped ring-buffer semantics; retained independent of entitlement state (hidden not deleted on lapse).

interfaces.name: HistoryEntry
interfaces.signature: struct HistoryEntry: Codable, Sendable, Identifiable { var id: UUID; var startedAt: Date; var endedAt: Date; var averageHealth: HealthLevel; var bottleneckRelayIDs: [UUID]; var summaryText: String }
interfaces.notes: Summary-grade diagnostics, not raw frame capture.

interfaces.name: RegionAccessState
interfaces.signature: enum RegionAccessState: Codable, Sendable { case unlockedEditable, lockedUpsell, restoredEmpty, frozenReadOnly }
interfaces.notes: Per-region UI-facing state, produced only by resolveRegionAccess (LOCKED) — not an ad hoc reduction any lane reinvents.

interfaces.name: SimulationEngine
interfaces.signature: protocol SimulationEngine: Sendable { func derive(_ topology: RegionTopology) -> NetworkDerivedState; func previewPlace(type: RelayType, at: Coordinate, in topology: RegionTopology, credits: Decimal) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func previewMove(relayID: UUID, to: Coordinate, in topology: RegionTopology) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func previewRetune(relayID: UUID, delta: Int, in topology: RegionTopology) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func previewUpgrade(relayID: UUID, in topology: RegionTopology, credits: Decimal) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func commitPlace(type: RelayType, at: Coordinate, in topology: inout RegionTopology, credits: inout Decimal) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func commitMove(relayID: UUID, to: Coordinate, in topology: inout RegionTopology) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func commitRetune(relayID: UUID, delta: Int, in topology: inout RegionTopology) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func commitUpgrade(relayID: UUID, in topology: inout RegionTopology, credits: inout Decimal) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func resolveOffline(topology: RegionTopology, lastActive: Date, now: Date, policy: OfflinePolicy) -> OfflineSummary }
interfaces.notes: Foundation-only, zero SwiftUI/UIKit imports, never calls Date() internally. Preview covers all four action kinds. This protocol wraps something genuinely worth swapping (deterministic vs. stub math), which is why it — unlike NetworkEngine — earns a protocol.

interfaces.name: NetworkMutationAction
interfaces.signature: enum NetworkMutationAction: Sendable { case placeRelay(regionID: RegionID, type: RelayType, position: CGPointCodable); case moveRelay(regionID: RegionID, relayID: RelayID, position: CGPointCodable); case retuneRelay(regionID: RegionID, relayID: RelayID, tuning: RelayTuning); case upgradeRelay(regionID: RegionID, relayID: RelayID) }
interfaces.notes: Single action surface for touch and accessibility paths.

interfaces.name: MutationResult
interfaces.signature: struct MutationResult: Sendable { var snapshot: GameSnapshot; var derived: NetworkDerivedState; var outcome: MutationOutcome }
interfaces.notes: Returned synchronously by engine-facing mutation entrypoints.

interfaces.name: PlacementPreview
interfaces.signature: struct PlacementPreview: Sendable { var isValid: Bool; var invalidReason: String?; var projectedCreditsDelta: Decimal; var projectedDerived: NetworkDerivedState }
interfaces.notes: Used by ghost placement UI and VoiceOver preview copy.

interfaces.name: MovePreview
interfaces.signature: struct MovePreview: Sendable { var isValid: Bool; var invalidReason: String?; var projectedDerived: NetworkDerivedState }
interfaces.notes: Same projection contract for drag moves and stepped accessibility actions.

interfaces.name: OfflinePolicy
interfaces.signature: struct OfflinePolicy: Sendable { var driftFloorSeconds: TimeInterval; var capSecondsFree: TimeInterval; var capSecondsPro: TimeInterval }
interfaces.notes: Explicit injected policy object so 8h/400d tests are deterministic.

interfaces.name: SnapshotStore
interfaces.signature: protocol SnapshotStore { func load() throws -> LoadedSnapshot; func save(_ snapshot: GameSnapshot) throws; func markLastActive(at date: Date) throws; func recoverFromCorruption() throws -> LoadedSnapshot }
interfaces.notes: Owns atomic writes, backup rotation, and broken-file quarantine.

interfaces.name: LoadedSnapshot
interfaces.signature: struct LoadedSnapshot: Sendable { var snapshot: GameSnapshot; var recoveryMessage: String? }
interfaces.notes: Lets UI show humane recovery banner when needed.

interfaces.name: EntitlementService
interfaces.signature: protocol EntitlementService { func currentProStatus() async -> ProStatus; func restorePurchases() async throws -> ProStatus }
interfaces.notes: Only service that touches StoreKit. Gameplay consumes reduced ProStatus only.

interfaces.name: ProStatus
interfaces.signature: enum ProStatus: Sendable { case notOwned; case active(source: ProSource); case restoredButNoLocalRegionData(source: ProSource); case lapsedFrozen }
interfaces.notes: Reduced entitlement state after combining StoreKit and local region-two presence.

interfaces.name: Clock
interfaces.signature: protocol Clock { var now: Date { get } var monotonicUptime: TimeInterval { get } }
interfaces.notes: Injectable time source for offline math and save timestamps.

interfaces.name: GameEngine
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable final class GameEngine: NetworkActioning { private(set) var snapshot: GameSnapshot; private(set) var derived: NetworkDerivedState; private(set) var entitlement: EntitlementState; private(set) var regionAccess: [RegionID: RegionAccessState]; private(set) var isReady: Bool; var presentedSurface: PresentedSurface?; var mapOverlay: MapOverlay?; func bootstrap() async; func handleScenePhaseChange(_ phase: ScenePhase) async; func attemptPlace(type: RelayType, at: Coordinate) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func attemptMove(relayID: UUID, to: Coordinate) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func attemptRetune(relayID: UUID, delta: Int) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func attemptUpgrade(relayID: UUID) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func present(_ surface: PresentedSurface?); func restorePurchases() async }
interfaces.notes: MUST be a final class, not a struct — settled in round 2. @Observable's single-shared-instance guarantee across map/sheet/progression/paywall/settings requires reference semantics for @Environment(GameEngine.self) injection; a mutating-struct engine would let a sheet read a stale copy of health/entitlement. attempt* methods are fully synchronous end-to-end, no await between validate and mutate.

interfaces.name: PresentedSurface
interfaces.signature: enum PresentedSurface: Identifiable, Equatable { case buildSheet(BuildSheetMode); case progression; case paywall; case settings }
interfaces.notes: Keeps map + bottom sheet as one continuous decision surface; has a slot for progression that an earlier 4-case draft lacked.

interfaces.name: NetworkCanvasModel
interfaces.signature: struct NetworkCanvasModel: Sendable { var topology: RegionTopology; var derived: NetworkDerivedState; var preview: PlacementOutcome?; var animationDate: Date }
interfaces.notes: Plain data input to the single Canvas + TimelineView(.animation) render surface; never a ForEach of individually animated shapes.

interfaces.name: HistoryRetentionService
interfaces.signature: protocol HistoryRetentionService: Sendable { func append(_ entry: HistoryEntry, to buffer: ProHistoryBuffer) -> ProHistoryBuffer }
interfaces.notes: Keeps Pro diagnostics bounded regardless of entitlement duration.

interfaces.name: SaveRecoveryBannerModel
interfaces.signature: struct SaveRecoveryBannerModel: Sendable { var message: String; var severity: RecoverySeverity }
interfaces.notes: Shared recovery UX payload from storage to UI.

interfaces.name: AccessibilityRelayActionFactory
interfaces.signature: func makeRelayAccessibilityActions(relayID: UUID, engine: any NetworkActioning) -> [UIAccessibilityCustomAction]
interfaces.notes: Must route through the same attempt*/preview engine entry points as the drag path, never duplicate placement/interference math.

interfaces.name: Coordinate
interfaces.signature: struct Coordinate: Codable, Hashable, Sendable { var x: Int; var y: Int }
interfaces.notes: Discrete tile-grid position; the only authoritative position type in RegionTopology/GameSnapshot. Continuous on-screen motion during an in-flight drag is view-local only, snapping to a tile on release before calling attemptMove once.

interfaces.name: RegionBounds
interfaces.signature: struct RegionBounds: Codable, Sendable { var width: Int; var height: Int }
interfaces.notes: Fixed per region; no pan/zoom camera in MVP.

interfaces.name: RegionID
interfaces.signature: enum RegionID: String, Codable, Sendable { case primary, secondary }
interfaces.notes: secondary is the Pro-gated region.

interfaces.name: RegionTopology
interfaces.signature: struct RegionTopology: Codable, Sendable { var regionID: RegionID; var bounds: RegionBounds; var relays: [Relay]; var demandZones: [DemandZone]; var unlockedMilestones: Set<String> }
interfaces.notes: Canonical per-region topology; health/throughput always recomputed from this, never persisted.

interfaces.name: HealthLevel
interfaces.signature: enum HealthLevel: String, Codable, Sendable { case healthy, strained, choking }
interfaces.notes: Always paired with icon/text; never color-only.

interfaces.name: NetworkHealthSnapshot
interfaces.signature: struct NetworkHealthSnapshot: Sendable { var level: HealthLevel; var score: Double; var bottleneckRelayIDs: [UUID] }
interfaces.notes: The single computed model read by map meter, progression screen, and VoiceOver delta previews — never cached separately.

interfaces.name: ClockProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol ClockProviding: Sendable { func now() -> Date; func monotonicUptime() -> TimeInterval }
interfaces.notes: Injected everywhere; simulation and engine never call Date() directly.

interfaces.name: ElapsedResolution
interfaces.signature: struct ElapsedResolution: Sendable { var effectiveSeconds: TimeInterval; var wasFloored: Bool; var wasCapped: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Encodes the two-threshold clock rule.

interfaces.name: SimulationCore
interfaces.signature: enum SimulationCore { static func computeHealth(_ topology: RegionTopology) -> NetworkHealthSnapshot; static func pairwiseInterference(distance: Double, relayRadius: Double) -> Double; static func resolveElapsed(lastActive: Date, now: Date, floorThreshold: TimeInterval, cap: TimeInterval) -> ElapsedResolution; static func offlineEarnings(steadyStateRate: Double, resolution: ElapsedResolution) -> Decimal }
interfaces.notes: Pure, framework-free, lives in the LongwaveSimulation package (Foundation-only dependency).

interfaces.name: PlacementError
interfaces.signature: enum PlacementError: Error, Sendable { case tooClose, occupiedByDemandZone, insufficientFunds, outOfBounds, unknownRelay }
interfaces.notes: Every case maps to a persistent, spoken (not flash) reason string, wired in polish_resilience.

interfaces.name: PlacementOutcome
interfaces.signature: struct PlacementOutcome: Sendable { var before: NetworkDerivedState; var after: NetworkDerivedState; var currencyDelta: Decimal }
interfaces.notes: Feeds the visual before/after delta chip and the spoken VoiceOver delta preview from one value, never two calculations.

interfaces.name: NetworkActioning
interfaces.signature: protocol NetworkActioning: AnyObject { func attemptPlace(type: RelayType, at: Coordinate) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func attemptMove(relayID: UUID, to: Coordinate) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func attemptRetune(relayID: UUID, delta: Int) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func attemptUpgrade(relayID: UUID) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func preview(_ action: PendingAction) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError> }
interfaces.notes: Drag, tap, confirm sheet, and VoiceOver custom actions are all thin callers into the same conforming NetworkEngine instance.

interfaces.name: MilestoneState
interfaces.signature: struct MilestoneState: Codable, Sendable { var firstRelayPlaced: Bool; var firstInterferenceResolved: Bool; var thirdGoalUnlocked: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Backs the progression screen's checklist rows.

interfaces.name: HistoryEvent
interfaces.signature: struct HistoryEvent: Codable, Sendable { var timestamp: Date; var kind: String; var healthDelta: Double }
interfaces.notes: Capped ring buffer, retained independent of entitlement state (hidden not deleted on lapse).

interfaces.name: OfflineSummary
interfaces.signature: struct OfflineSummary: Sendable { var elapsed: ElapsedResolution; var earnings: Decimal; var configurationChangedDuringGap: Bool }
interfaces.notes: configurationChangedDuringGap=false collapses the Pro history view to one flat entry.

interfaces.name: EntitlementState
interfaces.signature: enum EntitlementState: Equatable, Sendable { case notOwned, active, restoredButLocallyEmpty, lapsedFrozen }
interfaces.notes: Global StoreKit-derived Pro status. restoredButLocallyEmpty derived from local region-two save presence, not receipt presence alone.

interfaces.name: NetworkEngine
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable final class NetworkEngine: NetworkActioning { private(set) var snapshot: GameSnapshot; private(set) var derived: NetworkDerivedState; private(set) var entitlement: EntitlementState; private(set) var regionAccess: [RegionID: RegionAccessState]; private(set) var isReady: Bool; var presentedSurface: PresentedSurface?; var mapOverlay: MapOverlay?; init(simulation: SimulationEngine, storage: SnapshotStoring, entitlementService: EntitlementProviding, clock: ClockProviding); init(fixtureSnapshot: GameSnapshot, fixtureDerived: NetworkDerivedState, fixtureEntitlement: EntitlementState, fixtureRegionAccess: [RegionID: RegionAccessState], simulation: SimulationEngine, storage: SnapshotStoring, entitlementService: EntitlementProviding, clock: ClockProviding); func bootstrap() async; func handleScenePhaseChange(_ phase: ScenePhase) async; func attemptPlace(type: RelayType, at: Coordinate) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func attemptMove(relayID: UUID, to: Coordinate) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func attemptRetune(relayID: UUID, delta: Int) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func attemptUpgrade(relayID: UUID) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func preview(_ action: PendingAction) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func present(_ surface: PresentedSurface?); func showOverlay(_ overlay: MapOverlay?); func restorePurchases() async }
interfaces.notes: FINAL DECISION: single concrete final class, no separate protocol/LiveNetworkEngine split. Every real system boundary (StoreKit, FileManager, clock) is already abstracted one level down via SimulationEngine/SnapshotStoring/EntitlementProviding/ClockProviding, so there's nothing on NetworkEngine's own boundary worth protocol-swapping. The fixture initializer gives Previews/XCTest/XCUITest fixture data directly without risking @Observable's fine-grained view-update tracking through a protocol existential. Injected everywhere via @Environment(NetworkEngine.self) — the concrete type, always. attempt* methods fully synchronous end-to-end, no await between validate and mutate; async bootstrap/scenePhase/restorePurchases never interleave with an in-flight synchronous mutation.

interfaces.name: BuildSheetMode
interfaces.signature: enum BuildSheetMode: Equatable { case build(Coordinate); case inspect(UUID); case confirm(PendingAction) }
interfaces.notes: Nested inside PresentedSurface.buildSheet.

interfaces.name: PendingAction
interfaces.signature: enum PendingAction: Equatable { case place(RelayType, Coordinate); case move(UUID, Coordinate); case retune(UUID, Int); case upgrade(UUID) }
interfaces.notes: retune's Int matches Relay.tuningLevel's single-lever shape.

interfaces.name: MapOverlay
interfaces.signature: enum MapOverlay: Equatable { case offlineReturn(OfflineSummary); case recoveryBanner(RecoveryMessage) }
interfaces.notes: Deliberately separate from PresentedSurface — non-blocking overlays must never be dismissible by opening a sheet.

interfaces.name: SnapshotStoring
interfaces.signature: protocol SnapshotStoring: Sendable { func load() throws -> LoadResult; func save(_ snapshot: GameSnapshot) throws; func markLastActive(at date: Date) throws }
interfaces.notes: Owns atomic temp-file-then-rename writes against current.json, backup rotation to lastKnownGood.json, and broken-file quarantine under a timestamped filename.

interfaces.name: LoadResult
interfaces.signature: struct LoadResult: Sendable { var snapshot: GameSnapshot; var recoveryNotice: RecoveryNotice? }
interfaces.notes: Forces the caller to handle recovery presentation rather than silently swallowing it.

interfaces.name: RecoveryNotice
interfaces.signature: enum RecoveryNotice: Sendable { case rolledBackToLastKnownGood(preservedFilename: String); case rolledBackToCleanNetwork(preservedFilename: String) }
interfaces.notes: Corrupted file always preserved under a timestamped filename before fallback.

interfaces.name: EntitlementProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol EntitlementProviding: Sendable { var state: EntitlementState { get }; func refresh() async; func restore() async throws; func hasLocalRegionTwoData() -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: Only service touching StoreKit; checks both lifetime and subscription product IDs so either satisfies Pro; local transaction cache only, never blocks on network.

interfaces.name: RecoveryMessage
interfaces.signature: struct RecoveryMessage: Equatable, Sendable { var plainLanguageText: String; var notice: RecoveryNotice }
interfaces.notes: UI-facing calm-language translation of RecoveryNotice for the map banner and settings screen.

interfaces.name: VoiceOverDelta
interfaces.signature: struct VoiceOverDelta: Sendable { var outcome: PlacementOutcome; var announcement: String }
interfaces.notes: Spoken form of PlacementOutcome generated from the same value the visual delta chip uses — never a separate calculation.

interfaces.name: MilestoneChecklistItem
interfaces.signature: struct MilestoneChecklistItem: Identifiable { var id: String; var title: String; var isComplete: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Derived view of MilestoneState for the progression screen.

interfaces.name: RelayMetric
interfaces.signature: struct RelayMetric: Sendable { var relayID: UUID; var localThroughput: Double; var interferenceContribution: Double }
interfaces.notes: Per-relay breakdown feeding map labels and Pro bottleneck history.

interfaces.name: MapInteractionNode
interfaces.signature: struct MapInteractionNode: Identifiable, Sendable { var id: UUID; var coordinate: Coordinate; var kind: String }
interfaces.notes: Invisible SwiftUI hit-testing/accessibility layer, one per relay/demand-zone, positioned via screenPosition.

interfaces.name: screenPosition
interfaces.signature: func screenPosition(for coordinate: Coordinate, bounds: RegionBounds, viewportSize: CGSize) -> CGPoint
interfaces.notes: Single shared grid-to-screen conversion used by the Canvas renderer AND MapInteractionNode's hit-testing/accessibility layer, so the visible relay dot and its tap target can never drift apart on any screen size.

interfaces.name: LiveNetworkEngine
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable final class LiveNetworkEngine: NetworkEngine { private(set) var snapshot: GameSnapshot; private(set) var derived: NetworkDerivedState; private(set) var entitlement: EntitlementState; private(set) var regionAccess: [RegionID: RegionAccessState]; private(set) var isReady: Bool; var presentedSurface: PresentedSurface?; var mapOverlay: MapOverlay?; func bootstrap() async; func handleScenePhaseChange(_ phase: ScenePhase) async; func attemptPlace(type: RelayType, at: Coordinate) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func attemptMove(relayID: UUID, to: Coordinate) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func attemptRetune(relayID: UUID, delta: Int) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func attemptUpgrade(relayID: UUID) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func preview(_ action: PendingAction) -> Result<PlacementOutcome, PlacementError>; func present(_ surface: PresentedSurface?); func showOverlay(_ overlay: MapOverlay?); func restorePurchases() async }
interfaces.notes: Concrete final observable engine injected into every SwiftUI view via `@Environment(LiveNetworkEngine.self)`. `attempt*` methods are fully synchronous end-to-end, with no await between validation and mutation, and must not interleave concurrent state mutation from bootstrap, scene-phase, or restore flows.

interfaces.name: resolveRegionAccess
interfaces.signature: func resolveRegionAccess(entitlement: EntitlementState, hasLocalData: Bool) -> RegionAccessState
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: the single named reduction from global EntitlementState + per-device hasLocalRegionTwoData() into the per-region RegionAccessState the Pro screen and region-two map render. First test to write against the final contract: a restore-state matrix proving all entitlement × local-data combinations map correctly.

verify_summary: FAILED (compile FAILED for the iOS Simulator)
